Appearances: Don Zoellner appeared by telephone for Joseph Gill (Administrator).
Maurice Pierre in Propria Persona.

Mr. Pierre stated that he received the Nissan and that he understands the Administrator has reduced his fees by $7,000.00. The Court questions whether the parties want to submit the matter on what has been presented or if they want an evidentiary hearing. Mr. Pierre made reference to his amended objections, raised issue with the accounting and the funeral expenses, stated that there is a need for interrogatories and depositions, and that after the checks were cut, there has been no communication with Mr. Zoellner. Mr. Zoellner stated that there was no mediation, that they had agreed on a Mediator, but she did not get back to him, and that he had an accident and is on disability until January 25th and may require additional surgery. Mediation must be completed no later than January 9, 2019. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by January 16, 2019. The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on January 23, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Sep-10-2018 Continued To Nov-05-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Don Zoellner appeared by telephone for Joseph Gill (Administrator).
Maurice Pierre in Propria Persona.
Darryl Reed in Propria Persona.
Brenda Reed in Propria Persona.

Mr. Pierre stated that he received the tangible personal property, that he did not sign a receipt because he was never given one and that he is still objecting to the accounting. Mr. Pierre also stated that he was willing to sign the settlement, but he was not willing to waive his rights and that he believes there are legal issue that were not presented by Chaune Williams. Mr. Zoellner stated that the receipt was provided to Ms. Williams as well as Mr. Pierre, that he thought there was a global settlement, that he prepared the paper work and that everybody signed it except Mr. Pierre. The Court noted that Mr. Pierre's objection is to Mr. Gill's handling of the Estate, stated that it does not see that Mr. Pierre is recovering anything from Mr. Reed or Ms. Reed, and that it sees no reason to hold up their distribution. The Court explained to Mr. Pierre that if he goes to trial and he loses, he will have to pay the attorney's fees out of his share. Mr. Reed spoke of the services and care he provided for his Mother. Ms. Reed stated that she wants this to be over. The Court directed Mr. Zoellner to file a petition for preliminary distribution ($200,000.00 to Darryl Reed/$200,000.00 to Brenda Reed/$100,000.00 to Maurice Pierre). Mr. Zoellner informed the Court that there is a lien against Mr. Pierre by Ms. Williams, stated that there is no amount listed in the lien and that he does not want his Client to be sued for not honoring the lien. Mr. Pierre stated that Ms. Williams sent him a bill for $9,000.00, that he already gave her $7,000.00, that he is objecting to the lien and that he will file a change of address. The Court noted that there is still enough left over to cover it. Mr. Zoellner and Mr. Pierre agreed to participate in mediation. The Court signed the order referring the matter to Pro Bono Mediation. Mediation must be completed no later than October 25, 2018. The Parties are to notify the Probate Examiner as to the outcome of mediation by October 29, 2018. The First and Final Account of Administrator, etc. is continued to and the Parties are to return on November 5, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of mediation. Mr. Zoellner is to file his petition within seven days.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

P.S.
The Court will order a partial distribution at this time and continue the rest of the petition to November 5, 2018.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Don Zoellner appeared by telephone for Joseph Gill (Administrator).
Chaune Williams appeared by telephone for Maurice Pierre (Beneficiary).
Darryl Reed (Son of Decedent) in Propria Persona.
Brenda Reed (Daughter of Decedent) in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that this matter has been continued for a while now to find out if the Beneficiary was able to do a deal to purchase the property. Mr. Zoellner stated that the property was re-appraised at $910,000.00, that over the weekend he learned that Brenda and Darryl are willing to sell the house to Maurice Pierre at $867,500.00, that the only offer received by Mr. Pierre is $795,000.00 and that they have a listing agreement that is ready to go if the Beneficiaries can't agree. Ms. Williams stated that her Client feels he should be able to purchase the house close to date of death value, that her Client was not given notice, that he tried to reach out to his Siblings and spoke of the relationship between her Client and Mr. and Ms. Reed. Ms. Williams also stated that a Sibling was living on the property, that her Client looked at lower comps from Mr. Zoellners' Real Estate Agent, that her Client feels he could communicate with his Siblings if the Attorneys/Court were not involved and that she is prepared to file a civil action. The Court stated that the Parties could list the property, see what offers they get and Ms. William's Client can participate, or the Administrator can distribute in kind, and that there either is a deal or there isn't. The Court also stated that it wants the Estate to get closed. Mr. Zoellner stated that they can't distribute in kind because there is insufficient cash in the Estate to pay the two creditors and Estate expenses, and that there is a mortgage of $105,600.00 with a monthly payment of $882.00. Mr. Reed stated that they want to sell the house and not take ownership, that his Sister needs to find a safer place to live and that his concern is his Sister's well being. Status on close of estate is continued one last time on this status hearing to October 17, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a petition to do something.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-22-2016 Continued To Jan-10-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Maurice Pierre in Propria Persona. Pamela Kong for and with (proposed fiduciary) Paula Lawrence. Don Zoellner for (administrator) Joseph Gill. Darryl Reed & Brenda Reed in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that petitions have been filed regarding the removal of Mr. Gill and the appointment of Mr. Pierre, that it thought the parties were on course for a petition to appoint Ms. Lawrence but it has not been filed and that nothing is ready to go forward. Ms. Kong stated that they are ready to file their petition. Mr. Pierre stated that he objects to their filing a petition, that he has spoken to Ms. Lawrence, that he and his siblings were to talk about mediation, that he was sandbagged by his brother and then communication was cut off. The Court stated that there are two separate issues, one is who is going to administer the estate, that mediation is great if the parties want to do so and asked Mr. Pierre if he has objections to Ms. Lawrence replacing Mr. Gill. Mr. Pierre responded yes. The Court stated that there is no pending petition for removal due to procedural issues and stated that Mr. Pierre will not be appointed. Mr. Zoellner stated that he had prepared a stipulation in which Mr. Gill will resign and that everyone has signed it except Mr. Pierre. The Court stated that it encourages the parties to agree on a neutral administrator but that it cannot force it. Mr. Reed gave the history of how Mr. Gill came to be appointed, that it is what their mother wanted and stated that he has to keep taking off work to attend these hearings. Mr. Zoellner stated that this estate could have already been done, that they need to dispose of the personal property, sell the real property and then file the final account and that it could have been done by now. Mr. Pierre stated that disposing of the personal property could have been done, that Darryl is living in the property and is disposing of accounts. The Court stated that everyone has a different position, that it does not want any personal attacks in the courtroom and that it would be good to have a private fiduciary. Mr. Pierre stated that there needs to be an investigation as to what has happened. The Court stated that it could be done with a neutral in place and that the track Mr. Pierre has chosen will not succeed. Mr. Pierre stated that he is willing to have someone step in and that there is interference with the inheritance. The Court stated that a neutral could administer without being personally involved, that if Mr. Pierre signs the stipulation they can then submit the appropriate documents, that it encourages Mr. Pierre to get legal counsel to know what his rights are in this process and it may then be the time to talk about mediation. Ms. Reed stated that she wants Mr. Gill to remain as administrator and that she does not know what is going on. The court clarified what is happening or could happen to Ms. Reed. Mr. Pierre stated that he plans to get counsel and will perfect his petition so that it can go forward. The Court stated that it is talking about Ms. Lawrence becoming the administrator, that if it is not going to happen right away it will be a different scenario, asked Mr. Pierre when he can realistically act on this and that until the others know his decision they do not know how to proceed. Mr. Pierre stated that he does not know as the holidays are coming up. The Court stated that until that time Mr. Gill is still the administrator and can do what he wants. The Petition for Letters of Administration and the Petition for orders to remove respondent Joseph Gill as administrator, etc., were continued to January 10, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the signing of the stipulation and for a petition to bring Ms. Lawrence in or an explanation of how to resolve the issues. The Court encouraged Darryl & Brenda Reed to obtain legal counsel.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez/Yolanda Mazariegos. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Oct-11-2016 Continued To Nov-22-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Maurice Pierre in Propria Persona. Don Zoellner (via Court Call) for (administrator) Joseph Gill. Darryl Reed and Brenda Reed (children of decedent) also present.

The Court stated that everyone had been on course to choose a neutral professional to serve as administrator and asked where the parties stand. Mr. Pierre stated that he was referred to Herb Thomas, that Mr. Thomas was busy and referred him to Paula Lawrence, that he has not been able to contact Ms. Lawrence, that other fiduciaries have declined, that he is trying to comply with the examiner's notes, that the service that is helping him has not complied with the rules and used improper forms and that he is looking to use a different service, that there are issues regarding the bond and that in regards to Paula Lawrence he believes that they will be able to move forward. Mr. Zoellner reported that he spoke with Ms. Lawrence and she is ready to go, that he has provided the case information to her and suggested that she speak with Mr. Pierre. The Court stated that there is an issue with Mr. Gill being properly bonded. Mr. Zoellner stated that there is a mortgage against the property and that he is properly bonded. The Court explained the process, stated that there needs to be an increase for $170,000.00 and that absent that happening his client will be suspended. Mr. Zoellner stated that Ms. Lawrence will need 45 days to file her petition and publish notice. Mr. Pierre stated that the matter that was once part of the petition will be moot, that parts will still need to be addressed due to loss, that he will need to file an amendment and asked if they should mediate now or wait. The Court stated that if all parties want to mediate the Court will facilitate it, that they need an administrator in place and that it encourages everyone to reach an agreement. Ms. Reed stated that she would like her brother to take care of her needs as her mother did. The Court stated that her brother could not represent her interests or appear for her as he is not an attorney and advised the Reed's to get an attorney. The Petition for Letters of Administration and the Petition for order to remove respondent Joseph Gill as administrator, etc., were continued to November 22, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court expects that an ex parte regarding the bond will be filed, that Mr. Pierre will file his amended petition and talk to Ms. Lawrence and that her petition will be on file.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Maurice Pierre (via court call) in Propria Persona. Don Zoellner (via court call) for (administrator) Joseph Gill. Darryl Reed in Propria Persona. Brenda Reed in Propria Persona.

The Court stated that there are two petitions filed by Mr. Pierre, one that includes the removal of Mr. Gill and one seeking appointment, that neither are in a position to go forward, that proof of service on Judicial Council forms has not been filed, that there is nothing indicating that the bonding company was served, that Mr. Gill is prepared to resign if the parties are in favor of a professional being appointed, that regarding the merits it is very unlikely that the court will appoint Mr. Pierre due to the family issues and that no one has petitioned for replacement with a neutral third party. Mr. Zoellner stated that Mr. Pierre does not want his Uncle to serve, that he spoke with former attorney Mr. Myers regarding a professional being appointed and suggested Herb Thomas who is in agreement and that Mr. Thomas is reluctant to file a petition without the Court's agreement. The Court stated that it has no problem with a neutral third party being appointed. Mr. Pierre stated that they have spoken about this, that he is not aware of Herb Thomas except for a conversation with Mr. Zoellner, that he is looking for counsel, that he is concerned with the way the case has come about, that he is attempting to work with the family regarding the undue influence, that he is not opposed to co-administration or a neutral party and that he has spoken with Jacob Smith and Lawrence Leung and that they are in agreement. The Court stated that if the parties agree on a neutral third party the resignation and appointment can be done in one petition. Mr. Zoellner stated that on August 5, 2016 he spoke with Mr. Pierre, that Mr. Pierre is trying to find a professional that will co-administer with him and that he does not think a professional will agree to this. The Court agreed that it sounds unlikely, that given the state of this matter it is unlikely that the Court would appoint Mr. Pierre, that if the parties want to mediate the Court will support it and that this case qualifies for Pro Bono Mediation. Mr. Zoellner stated that he has no problem with mediation, that the only issue to decide is who is going to administer the estate and that they should be able to come to an agreement regarding a professional without the time and money spent on mediation. The Court stated that it will continue the matter to let the parties see if they can reach an agreement regarding administration at which time Mr. Pierre's petitions can go off calendar, that if they cannot reach an agreement he will need to correct the deficiencies and that it will continue for the parties to try and work it out without further fighting in Court. Mr. Reed asked if it was possible to come to an agreement today. The Court stated that it sounds like everyone is on board, that there needs to be an agreement on a person, that paperwork will need to be filed and that it encourages everyone to speak to each other. Mr. Zoellner stated that he is confident that they can agree on someone. Mr. Pierre asked if the Court was ordering him to agree with them and asked if it can order mediation. The Court stated that it cannot order him to agree, that it will not order mediation when all parties are not ready to go into it and that he has the ability to dismiss his petitions at any time. Mr. Zoellner asked if they submitted a stipulation prior to the continuance date would they need to appear. The Court stated that it won't know until it is filed and that they may need a petition. The Petition for letters of administration and the petition for orders, etc., were continued to October 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)
